verdict.answer_first: A and B both lead with the same bottom line: "HTTPS is just HTTP running inside a TLS tunnel"; A is tighter because it immediately frames the 3 outcomes without later burying a recap.
verdict.decisiveness: A wins: it keeps one explanatory spine; B ends by offering three paths, "certificate chain validation, the Diffie-Hellman math, or how TLS 1.3 differs," which dilutes the close.
verdict.fabrication: B is more risky: "This also defeats replay and reordering" is over-broad without scope; A has no comparable unsupported expansion beyond standard TLS claims.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to save: "Certificates answer... Diffie-Hellman answers... AEAD cipher answers..." compresses the mechanism into a reusable prescription.
